description: Total number of directors currently on the Board.
notes: LLM-extracted; feeds board_independence_ratio's denominator. Observed coverage: 26.9% (ar_field_coverage.csv).

description: Number of those directors who are Independent Directors.
notes: Raw count, confounded by board_size -- a larger board mechanically has more independent members in absolute terms without a more independent composition; prefer board_independence_ratio for cross-firm comparison. Not separately tracked in ar_field_coverage.csv.

description: Independent directors / board_size (derived, not LLM-reported directly).
notes: Derived field (ar_extractor.py L302-303): null if board_size is None/0 or board_independent_count is None. Observed coverage: 26.9%.

description: Number of Board meetings held during the year.
notes: Genuinely contested sign in the literature -- more meetings can reflect either active oversight or firm distress requiring extra meetings. Observed coverage: 22.5%.

description: Average/overall attendance %, ONLY if the AR states this as an explicit summary figure (never computed by the LLM from a per-director table -- that is deliberately disallowed).
notes: Very sparse by design -- the LLM is barred from computing it itself, so most ARs yield null unless the report states a summary figure directly. Observed coverage: 0.2% -- excluded from 09_regression.ipynb's AR_VARS on this basis. Coverage sparsity, not signal absence.

description: % of promoter shareholding that is pledged/encumbered.
notes: "Nil"/"0%" is coded as a real 0, not null (ar_extractor.py prompt), so missingness reflects true non-disclosure, not non-pledging. Observed coverage: 4.0% -- excluded from AR_VARS (too sparse).

description: Aggregate related-party-transaction value for the year, normalized to INR Crore from whatever unit (Crore/Lakh/Thousand/actual-rupees) the AR itself reports.
notes: Scale before use -- raw crore value is size-driven, not comparable across firms of different size without normalizing (e.g. by revenue/market cap). Observed coverage: 2.1% -- excluded from AR_VARS (too sparse).

description: Statutory audit fee paid to the auditor for the year, normalized to INR Crore.
notes: Scale before use -- raw crore value is size-driven. Extraction has strict table-identification guardrails against grabbing an unrelated fee-table row (ar_extractor.py SYSTEM_PROMPT). Observed coverage: 53.4% -- the best-covered AR field, included in AR_VARS; showed the strongest raw significance among AR variables in 09_regression.ipynb Table B (one hit at raw p approx 4.9e-08) but did not survive Romano-Wolf correction.

description: Aggregate contingent liabilities figure, normalized to INR Crore.
notes: Scale before use -- raw crore value is size-driven. Observed coverage: 11.7% -- excluded from AR_VARS (too sparse).

description: Count of ALL director_change events that FY (appointed + resigned + ceased + reappointed) -- every event type counts, not just departures.
temporal_key: aggregated to firm-FY from event-level news_dt (via _to_indian_fiscal_quarter)
notes: Counts appointments/reappointments together with resignations/cessations -- an orderly succession and a pressured director exit both increase this count identically. Sign should be read with that conflation in mind, not as a pure "departures" or "turnover-under-duress" measure.

description: Count of credit_rating_change events whose direction is a genuine upgrade or downgrade; reaffirmations, first-time assignments, and withdrawals are excluded (not "changes" in rating level).
temporal_key: aggregated to firm-FY from event-level news_dt
notes: NETS upgrades and downgrades together (reg30_firm_fy_agg.py L175-176: "real_changes" excludes reaffirmed/assigned/withdrawn/unclear but never separates direction_cw=="upgrade" from "downgrade"). A firm with 2 upgrades and a firm with 2 downgrades get the identical count. The suggested "negative" direction is NOT reliable as-is -- split into n_upgrades/n_downgrades before treating sign as informative.

description: Binary 0/1 flag for any auditor change that FY -- not a count, since auditor changes are too sparse (~0.4/firm-FY) for a count to be meaningful under fixed effects.
temporal_key: aggregated to firm-FY from event-level news_dt
notes: Direction is conceptually ambiguous (routine mandatory rotation under the Companies Act vs. a for-cause change), but treated as an instability signal here consistent with the other Reg30 event counts.

description: 1 for every row -- all 100 sampled firms x all 3 FYs get a row; lets a downstream left-merge distinguish "covered, zero events" from "not in the Reg30 sample at all".
temporal_key: firm-FY (constant scaffold, not derived from news_dt)
notes: NOT a governance signal -- always 1 within this file. Only 100/247 firms are Reg30-covered at all, via a stratified (not missing-at-random) sample. Exists purely as a merge/coverage bookkeeping column.
